description: §14.8.9 egress redaction — a server fn returning SELECT * over a protect= table strips the protected column at the egress sink and fires I-PROTECT-STRIP-001 (info, names the stripped column). The compile-time half of the server-fn return-boundary redaction (item 2 codes / item 5). RUNTIME NOTE: the client-visible absence is NOT asserted here — the conformance adapter mocks the server (fetch-mock returns the serverStub verbatim), so the server-side redaction sink never runs; the runtime half is adapter-gated (see ss60.progress adapter-gap escalation).
rationale: §14.8.9: every compiler-emitted client-egress serializer strips by column-origin every protected column before it crosses to the client; the strip is never silent — I-PROTECT-STRIP-001 names each stripped column. A `<db protect=passwordHash>` table SELECTed with * and returned engages the redact sink at compile.
